message:
So there I was at 11 PM, smell of something burning in the kitchen, and I reach for my phone to call my mom for advice... dead battery. Completely dead. I stood there for like three seconds just staring at the black screen like it would magically help me.

Turned out it was just a pan I'd forgotten about (rookie move), but it hit me: what if I'd actually needed to call 911? What if my kids needed to reach me?

I've since made sure everyone knows our address by heart, keeps our escape plan printed and visible (not just on a phone somewhere), and I actually test our smoke alarms now instead of ignoring that beeping. Nothing fancy—just the basics that don't depend on WiFi or battery life going exactly right.

Tech is great until it isn't. The stuff that actually matters is the stuff you can do in the dark with dead devices.

Have you talked through a "what if communication fails" plan with your family yet?
